messages.content: You are helping build OpenChronicle, a modular interactive storytelling engine with flexible LLM integration options. OpenChronicle supports multiple deployment modes: self-contained Docker with optional embedded Ollama, local development with external LLMs, or cloud deployment with services like OpenAI. The system features intelligent content analysis, multi-model orchestration, and advanced memory management for seamless narrative continuity.

This project is OpenChronicle: a modular AI-driven storytelling engine designed for continuity-rich, interactive narrative experiences. It supports local or remote LLMs (OpenAI, Ollama, etc), user-owned storypacks, per-story memory/state management, rollback features, and extensible frontends like CLI or Discord bots.

Core engine modules include:

- scene_logger.py → logs scene_id, user/model input, memory state, flags, canon used
- story_loader.py → loads storypacks (canon, memory, characters, meta.yaml, style)
- context_builder.py → builds prompts using memory + canon + analysis + user input
- content_analyzer.py → uses LLM to classify tone, flags, route models
- memory_manager.py → stores/retrieves world state, character status, etc
- database.py → story-local SQLite persistence with rollback and memory tracking
- model_adapter.py → abstraction for different LLM providers (OpenAI, Ollama, mock)

Design goals:
- Modular
- Local-first but model-agnostic
- NSFW-safe routing
- Memory versioning and rollback
- Scene tracking and timeline export
- Docker and Synology-friendly

📊 PROJECT STATUS: Core Foundation Complete - Active Development Phase

Project Structure:
- /core/: main Python modules (8 foundational modules)
- /storypacks/: per-story folders with canon, characters, memory, and style guides
- /storage/: SQLite databases per story, with exports folder
- /config/: model configuration (models.json) with 15+ LLM providers

✅ COMPLETED CORE MODULES (Foundation Complete):
- story_loader.py: loads storypacks and parses meta.yaml with robust error handling
- scene_logger.py: logs scenes to SQLite with flags, memory snapshots, rollback support
- context_builder.py: builds prompts using current memory and canon snippets with analysis integration
- memory_manager.py: manages character memory, world state, flags, recent events via SQLite
- rollback_engine.py: complete rollback system with backup and integrity validation
- database.py: SQLite utilities, migration, stats, and table management
- model_adapter.py: unified LLM adapter system supporting 12+ providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Cohere, Groq, Ollama, Mistral, HuggingFace, Azure OpenAI, Stability AI, Replicate, mock) plus image generation
- content_analyzer.py: intelligent content analysis using local LLM for token optimization

🏗️ ARCHITECTURE HIGHLIGHTS:
- LLM Support: 12+ providers with unified interface (see config/models.json)
- Flexible deployment: Docker (with optional Ollama), local development, or cloud
- Optional two-tier LLM system: Local LLM (analysis) + Main LLM (generation)
- Each story gets its own SQLite database at storage/{story_id}/openchronicle.db
- Tables: scenes, memory, memory_history, rollback_points, rollback_backups
- Static content (canon, characters) remains in YAML/text files
- Dynamic data (scenes, memory, rollback) stored in SQLite
- Model adapters configured via config/models.json with extensive provider support
- Optional content analysis with token optimization (50-80% reduction)
- Centralized logging system with utilities for maintenance and monitoring

✅ COMPLETED FEATURES (Core Foundation):
- ✅ SQLite integration with all core modules
- ✅ LLM adapter system with 12+ providers (text + image generation)
- ✅ Intelligent content analysis system (optional local LLM)
- ✅ Flexible Docker deployment with optional embedded Ollama
- ✅ Optional two-tier LLM architecture for token optimization
- ✅ Smart context selection and token optimization
- ✅ Basic test suite (core systems validated)
- ✅ Configuration management system with extensive provider support
- ✅ Virtual environment setup with proper dependency management
- ✅ Multiple deployment options (Docker, local, cloud)
- ✅ Complete modularity - use any combination of features
- ✅ Clean, stable codebase foundation
- ✅ Centralized logging and utilities system
- ✅ CLI commands: memory, models, switch, rollback (functional in interactive mode)

🎯 KEY FEATURES (In Development):
- 🧠 Content Classification: Analyzes user input for type, intent, and entities (basic implementation)
- 🔧 Token Optimization: Reduces context size by 50-80% through smart selection (functional but needs refinement)
- 🏷️ Automatic Flagging: Generates memory flags from content analysis (basic implementation)
- 🎯 Smart Routing: Routes content to appropriate models based on analysis (basic implementation)
- 🔒 NSFW Detection: Content filtering and model routing for sensitive content (planned)
- 🐳 Self-Contained Option: Optional embedded Ollama with ~2GB Llama 3.2 models
- 💰 Cost-Effective Options: No API fees with local models, or pay-per-use with cloud
- 🔐 Privacy Options: Complete local processing available, or cloud integration
- 🔧 Flexible Integration: Works with 12+ LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, local models

📦 DEPLOYMENT OPTIONS (Basic Implementation):
- Docker with Embedded Ollama: `docker-compose up -d` (functional but needs refinement)
- Local Development: `.venv` with `pip install -r requirements.txt` + external LLM
- Cloud Deployment: Multi-provider integration with environment variables
- Hybrid: Mix local and cloud models as needed
- Cross-Platform: Windows, Linux, macOS support for all deployment modes

📦 Standardized Storypack Format + Converter Utility

🎯 Goals:
- Provide users with structured examples for building their own stories
- Enable conversion of loose files (markdowns, character bios, scenes) into OpenChronicle storypacks

📁 Storypack Structure:
- `meta.yaml`: story title, description, default model, tone lock, NSFW flags
- `characters/*.json`: each file holds a character with name, motivation, style_block, memory
- `canon/*.md`: core world lore documents
- `memory/*.json`: persistent state objects (world flags, scene progress, etc.)
- `style_guide.md`: optional, sets narrative constraints or style modes
- `scenes/*.jsonl`: logged user/model exchanges with memory state snapshots

🛠️ Converter Utility (`storypack_converter.py`):
- Input: folder of user-supplied files (bios, notes, markdown, etc.)
- Output: best-guess storypack structure
- Uses:
   - heuristics and keyword detection
   - optional LLM pass to classify tone or extract memory
   - interactive CLI prompt to name characters, label canon, etc.

📚 Templates Folder:
- Include `examples/` directory with 1–2 complete storypacks for user reference
- Examples: 'Smalltown Mystery', 'Fantasy Tavern Start', 'Cyberpunk Noir'

📁 File Impacts:
- `story_loader.py`: validate and enforce structure
- `storypack_converter.py`: new module for bootstrapping user files into OpenChronicle format
- `.copilot/examples/`: store example storypack components for Copilot awareness

📝 Style Guide Utilization + meta.yaml Role Definition

🎯 Goals:
- Actively use `style_guide.md` to influence model output and narrative format
- Define the structure and purpose of `meta.yaml` per storypack

📘 Style Guide Support:
- Parse `style_guide.md` in `story_loader.py`
- Extract fields such as:
   - narrative_mode: e.g. 'first-person present tense'
   - voice: e.g. 'sarcastic and verbose'
   - format: e.g. 'dialogue-heavy scene format with ### separators'
   - restrictions: e.g. 'no modern slang', 'avoid inner monologue'
- Inject style directives into prompt in `context_builder.py`
- Allow for optional locking or override of style per session
- Re-inject style on long time skips or POV switches

📄 meta.yaml Role:
- Defines global story-level metadata for a storypack
- Example fields:
   - title
   - description
   - author
   - default_model: openai, ollama, etc.
   - fallback_chain: [openai, ollama, mock]
   - style_guide: path to active style file
   - nsfw_flags: true/false
- Parsed by `story_loader.py` and injected into session config or runtime memory
- May override global engine settings on a per-story basis

✅ Model Configuration Enhancements (Plugin-Style) - COMPLETED

📁 Modular Config Structure: ✅ IMPLEMENTED
- Replaced single `models.json` with `/models/{model}.json` files
- Each file includes provider, endpoint, token limits, safety flags, etc.
- 8 providers fully configured: OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, Ollama, Cohere, Google, Mistral, Perplexity, Mock

📂 model_registry.json: ✅ IMPLEMENTED
- Defines active models, their priorities, and fallback chains
- Working configuration with comprehensive fallback chains
- Content routing rules for NSFW, analysis, creative, and fast model selection

🔁 Fallback Chain Handling: ✅ IMPLEMENTED
- Attempts each model in priority/fallback order until one succeeds
- Logs failures and successful fallback model used
- Throws error if all fail
- Automatic failover tested and working

🧪 Runtime Filters: ✅ IMPLEMENTED
- Uses `supports_nsfw`, `allowed_content`, `blocked_content` from model config
- Matches with `content_analyzer` output to determine routing eligibility
- Health check system for monitoring provider availability

🛠️ Example CLI/Meta Support: ✅ IMPLEMENTED
- Allows `meta.yaml` to define default/fallback models per story
- Plugin-style configuration loading with legacy fallback
- Backward compatibility maintained with existing `models.json`

📈 IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Enhanced ModelManager with plugin loading capabilities
- Comprehensive error handling and graceful fallbacks
- Health monitoring for all providers
- Content-based routing system
- Cost tracking and rate limiting support
- Full test coverage with working mock adapter

🔧 FILES CREATED/MODIFIED:
- `config/model_registry.json` - Central registry
- `config/models/[provider].json` - Individual provider configs (8 providers)
- `core/model_adapter.py` - Enhanced with plugin support
- `test_model_config.py` - Comprehensive test suite
- `MODEL_CONFIG_IMPLEMENTATION.md` - Complete documentation

🎯 NEXT STEPS: Move to next priority todo item from development list

🪙 Token Management and Scene Continuation Strategy

🎯 Goals:
- Avoid mid-response truncation
- Seamlessly chain scenes if model output exceeds token limits
- Proactively manage token usage across models

🧮 Token Awareness:
- Add token estimation logic to `context_builder.py`
- Use tokenizer (e.g., tiktoken, llama-tokenizer) to pre-calculate total prompt+response size
- Automatically trim memory/canon chunks if prompt exceeds limit

🔁 Scene Continuation:
- Detect likely truncation (incomplete sentence, unfinished quote, missing stop tokens)
- Retry continuation using trimmed context + `continue: true`
- Log all fragments under same `scene_id` with suffix (e.g., `scene_021a`, `021b`)

📦 Multi-Part Stitching:
- Allow multi-turn responses per character or narrator
- Use `scene_stitcher.py` (or equivalent) to merge and finalize scene output

📊 Token Usage Tracker:
- Add token usage counters per model in a shared `token_tracker.py`
- Monitor active tokens in use, soft limits, and reset cycles (for rate-limited APIs)
- Enable user warnings or soft fallback when usage thresholds are hit

🔃 Auto Model Fallback:
- If scene continuation fails, auto-switch to fallback model from chain
- Log both failure and success path for debug and replay

🎭 Character Tone and Style Continuity Across LLMs

🎯 Goals:
- Maintain immersion even when switching between LLM models mid-story
- Ensure character voice, motivation, syntax, and emotional state remain consistent

📜 Per-Character Style Blocks:
- Add `style_block` to each `character_profile.json`, e.g.:
  {
    "voice": "Dry humor. Speaks in clipped sentences.",
    "tone": "Guarded but poetic",
    "syntax": "Avoids contractions. Formal."
  }
- Inject this block into all prompts via `context_builder.py`
- Format differently per model (OpenAI, Ollama, etc.) but preserve semantics

🔁 Echo-Driven Scene Anchoring:
- When switching models or recovering a scene, inject recent mood/tone/memory snapshot
- Prevents sudden character shifts due to model change

🪢 Optional LLM Tone Auditor:
- Classify output tone per turn
- Score it against style block for deviation
- Trigger re-prompt or fallback if tone drifts too far

🧵 Narrative Stitching Layer (Optional):
- Final LLM merges character turns and smooths tone
- Useful if fanout model outputs need rephrasing or coherence tightening

📁 File Impacts:
- `character_profile.json`: Add `style_block`
- `context_builder.py`: Inject tone/style directives
- `model_adapter.py`: Adjust prompt format per engine
- `scene_stitcher.py`: (optional) Normalize tone post-response
- `tone_auditor.py`: (optional) Analyze consistency of character speech

🧩 Additional Developer Targets for OpenChronicle

🔖 Scene Labeling + Bookmarking:
- Add support for optional `scene_label` field in scene logger
- Enable timeline view or export with labeled chapters

🔍 Full-Text Search (FTS5):
- Enable SQLite FTS5 for searching memory and scenes
- Add CLI command or internal utility for indexed querying

📤 Publisher-Friendly Export:
- Allow exporting full story to Markdown or structured JSON
- Optionally embed metadata (e.g., scenes, characters, canon sources)

🪄 Style Override Support:
- Allow session-wide lock on style mode (narrative, dialogue, action)

🔁 LLM Fallback Logic:
- Add failover order: primary → secondary → mock/local
- Log which engine was used on fallback

🕰️ Memory Versioning:
- Store changes alongside `scene_id` for traceability
- Optional rollback to memory state at specific point

⏳ CLI Time Machine Tooling:
- Navigate backward in story using labels, timestamps, or hashes
- Provide story diffs for debugging or creative exploration

📉 Disk Guardrails:
- Add optional disk usage warning if container exceeds set threshold
- Useful for Raspberry Pi, NAS, or micro VPS deployments

🚧 MAJOR DEVELOPMENT PRIORITIES:
1. 📚 Comprehensive Documentation: User guides, API docs, setup instructions
2. 🎨 User Interface: Web UI or enhanced CLI for better user experience
3. 🧪 Extensive Testing: Unit tests, integration tests for all 12+ LLM providers
4. 🔄 Story Management: Import/export, migration, and backup tools
5. 📊 Analytics & Monitoring: Story progression tracking, performance metrics
6. 🔌 Plugin Architecture: Extensible system for custom features
7. 🌐 Internationalization: Multi-language support for global audiences
8. 🎮 Advanced Interactive Elements: Enhanced choice mechanics, branching narratives
9. 🛡️ Security & Validation: Input sanitization, content filtering, rate limiting
10. 📈 Performance Optimization: Caching, async operations, memory management
11. 🎯 User Experience: Onboarding, tutorials, error handling
12. 🔧 Configuration Management: GUI for model setup, story configuration
13. 🤖 LLM Provider Expansion: Add missing providers from context claims (Together AI, Perplexity, OpenRouter, Anyscale, DeepInfra, Botify LLM)
14. 📦 Story Pack System: Complete storypacks structure and management tools
15. 🔍 Content Classification: Finish NSFW detection and smart routing implementation
16. 🎭 Character Arc Management: Emotional tracking and development systems
17. 🌍 World State Persistence: Advanced world building and state management
18. 🔀 Branching Narratives: Complete choice mechanics and story branching
19. 📊 Model Orchestration: Multi-model coordination and routing logic
20. 🎯 Deployment Optimization: Raspberry Pi support, cloud optimization

⚠️ KNOWN LIMITATIONS & TODO:
- Basic error handling needs enhancement
- Limited user interface (mainly CLI)
- No comprehensive test coverage yet
- Documentation is minimal
- No user authentication or multi-user support
- Performance optimization needed for large stories
- Content filtering system incomplete
- No backup/restore functionality for user data
- Configuration could be more user-friendly
- Need better logging and debugging tools

🔍 README vs IMPLEMENTATION GAPS:
- Anthropic/Claude IS implemented (corrected from earlier assessment)
- Story pack management system described but incomplete
- Branching narrative mechanics mentioned but basic implementation
- Character arc and emotional tracking systems not implemented
- Content classification and NSFW detection partially implemented
- Model orchestration and smart routing basic implementation
- Raspberry Pi optimization not tested
- CLI commands (models, switch, memory, rollback) are implemented but only work in interactive mode
- Docker deployment functional but needs refinement and testing
- Cloud deployment guides incomplete
- Multi-model coordination system needs development
- Image generation capabilities exist but not documented in README
- Utilities system (logging, backup, maintenance) exists but not mentioned in README

📋 DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES:
- Don't hardcode file paths
- Keep each module focused and clean
- Think in story logic, not web app logic
- Support multiple LLM backends equally (all 12+ providers)
- Maintain SQLite-based architecture for performance
- Make content analysis and token optimization optional features
- Design for flexible deployment - local, cloud, or hybrid
- Present all integration options as equally valid choices
- Prioritize stability and user experience over feature complexity

🎉 CURRENT STATUS: Solid foundation with core architecture complete. Functional basic implementation ready for extensive development and feature enhancement. Significant work remaining before production readiness.
